_comment: Copy to voice.json (or pass --voice) to steer deck generation into your own register. Add a voice.md beside it for longer, free-form guidance. With no voice file, a neutral professional default is used. Everything below describes the shape of each field rather than proposing a style: replace all of it. Your register is the point, and a tool that shipped one would give every deck built with it the same voice.
name: My voice
audience: Who reads your decks. Be specific: how senior, how technical, and what they already know.
tone: How it should sound, in a few words.
forbidden: the words and constructions you never use
one per line, and concrete enough for a model to obey
a punctuation mark you dislike, or a rhetorical turn you find cheap
preferred: the habits you want on every slide
concrete again: a rule you could mark someone down for breaking
a sentence length, or a thing to lead with
instructions: Free-form guidance, in your own words, and where a house style actually lives: the fields above cannot hold a paragraph. Say what a slide is for, what you never put in writing and save for the call, and how you handle a number, a risk, or a competitor. Write it the way you would brief a colleague about to draft for your clients.
notes: Anything else. Appended to the prompt as written.
